title: trifolium calocephalum
intro: Trifolium calocephalum, also known as red-stemmed clover, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Trifolium calocephalum include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Trifolium calocephalum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as salves and creams, to support skin and wound healing.
